methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Limelight Steel is a pre-seed stage frontier-tech company in Climate & Energy founded by Olivia Dippo and Andy Zhao. The sector includes 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA), though a sufficiently narrow peer set at the same development stage and valuation tier does not exist to support multiples-based analysis. Top private peers in the sector include Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised to date ($6M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Pre-Seed to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $15M. This method anchors directly to actual investment and assumes normalized stage advancement dynamics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $17M (low to high), with a median of $15M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological input rather than cross-validation across multiple independent approaches. Key uncertainties include product-stage progress, capital efficiency relative to sector benchmarks, and the absence of direct valuation comparables at the pre-seed stage in this subsector. Any revision would require either demonstrated operational milestones or emergence of closer peer transaction data.
